skill.name: wiki-qa
skill.description: Answers questions about a code repository using source file analysis. Use when the user asks a question about how something works, wants to understand a component, or needs help navigating the codebase.
skill.summary: Answer repository questions with source-grounded explanations and inline citations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported two external command patterns and one weak cryptography pattern, but all three are false positives after context review. The flagged backticks are Markdown formatting examples, and the description line contains no cryptographic algorithm or execution behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Formatting
low_findings.description: The flagged text on line 25 describes how responses may use Markdown headings, code blocks, tables, and bullet lists. It is documentation text, not Ruby or shell command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks delimit Markdown syntax examples inside a bullet list. No executable code path, shell invocation, or user-controlled command construction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Citation Format Example
low_findings.description: The flagged text on line 26 shows an inline citation format using backticks. It is a formatting instruction and does not run commands or invoke a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains a literal citation example in Markdown. There is no command interpreter, subprocess call, or execution context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The flagged description line states the skill answers repository questions using source file analysis. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, or security-sensitive crypto usage appears on this line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review confirms the line is front matter prose, not code. No weak algorithm such as MD5, SHA-1, DES, or RC4 is referenced.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time when repository knowledge is scattered across files. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to answer questions using source evidence and citations.
actual_capabilities: Answers repository questions using only source file evidence.
Searches for relevant files before forming an answer.
Explains files, functions, components, and code relationships.
Provides inline citations with file paths and line references.
Lists key files and their roles when answering.
limitations: It does not add new runtime tools or automation.
It depends on the assistant having access to repository files.
It should not answer from external knowledge when source evidence is missing.
It cannot guarantee complete coverage of very large repositories.

use_cases.title: Onboard to an unfamiliar repository
use_cases.description: Ask how a feature works and receive an evidence-based map of the key files.

use_cases.title: Trace implementation details
use_cases.description: Locate where a behavior is defined and understand the source paths involved.

use_cases.title: Prepare technical documentation
use_cases.description: Turn code investigation into concise explanations with citations for review.

prompt_templates.title: Explain a feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use wiki-qa to explain how [feature name] works in this repository. Cite the source files you use.

prompt_templates.title: Find an implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use wiki-qa to find where [behavior or API] is implemented. Include the key files and their roles.

prompt_templates.title: Compare two components
prompt_templates.prompt: Use wiki-qa to compare [component A] and [component B]. Ground the answer in source evidence only.

prompt_templates.title: Build an architecture answer
prompt_templates.prompt: Use wiki-qa to explain the architecture behind [workflow]. Trace the source path, data flow, and missing evidence.

output_examples.input: How does authentication work in this project?
output_examples.output: A concise source-grounded explanation with cited files, a key files table, and a note about any missing evidence.

output_examples.input: Where is the import pipeline defined?
output_examples.output: A file-by-file map of the pipeline entry point, supporting modules, and cited lines that support the answer.

output_examples.input: Compare the admin and public routing paths.
output_examples.output: A structured comparison that names each relevant route file, explains responsibilities, and avoids unsupported guesses.

best_practices: Ask focused questions about one feature, workflow, or component at a time.
Request citations when the answer will be used for documentation or review.
Provide the repository context or target directory when the codebase is large.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for questions that require external product or market knowledge.
Do not accept answers that lack source citations for factual claims.
Do not ask it to modify code; this skill is for repository Q&A.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an assistant answer questions about a repository using source file analysis and citations.

faq.question: Does it execute code?
faq.answer: No. The skill only provides instructions for reading files, searching code, and answering with evidence.

faq.question: Can it answer without citations?
faq.answer: The intended format includes inline citations and a key files table whenever source evidence is available.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it useful for onboarding?
faq.answer: Yes. It can explain how features are organized and identify the files a new developer should read.

faq.question: What should I do if evidence is missing?
faq.answer: Ask the assistant to state what is missing and suggest the next files or directories to inspect.
